---
title: "Machine Learning Projects"
description: "Applying data science and predictive modeling to optimize athletic performance and biomechanics."
toc: true
---

---

## Predicting Velocity with Force Plates: Can Lower Body Forces Predict Velocity on the Mound?

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Objective:** To determine if lower-body mechanics and force output from a Countermovement Jump (CMJ) can accurately predict a pitcher's throwing velocity using machine learning regression models. 
:::

**Tools & Technologies:** `R`, `Machine Learning (Regression)`, `Hawkin Dynamics Force Plates`

### Project Breakdown

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### ️ Context & Data
Lower body strength and force output are key factors in building the body to handle the demands of throwing. Working at the **Kinetic Performance Institute (KPI)** in California, I utilized athlete monitoring data derived from daily Countermovement Jumps (CMJ). The goal was to take complex, multi-variable force plate data and translate it into a predictive profile for on-mound velocity.

####  Methodology
The initial dataset contained over **79 potential force plate metrics**. Using feature selection and dimensionality reduction techniques, the model isolated the variables most heavily correlated with throwing velocity to reduce noise and prevent overfitting. 

The models were evaluated based on their Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) to determine how closely they could predict a pitcher's actual fastball velocity based purely on their lower-body jump mechanics.

#### Key Findings
The final model produced an **RMSE of 6.31**, meaning it predicts throwing velocity within about ± 6.3 mph on average. While there is room to improve absolute accuracy, the model successfully identified the **7 most critical metrics** for velocity prediction. 

The top drivers of velocity included:
* **Flight Time & Average Propulsive Power:** Dictates how much force the athlete produces against the ground and how efficiently they move that load.
* **Stiffness & Average Braking Power:** Relates to how well an athlete absorbs force and rapidly transitions to upward motion.

**Training Takeaway:** Coaches should prioritize drop-based plyometrics and overcoming isometrics to maximize change of momentum and force capacity during acceleration.

#### 📄 Reports & Code
Explore the full methodology, code, and non-technical breakdowns below:

* [**Read the Full Technical Report (HTML)**](data/final_report.html)
* [**View the Repository on GitHub**](https://github.com/yourusername/repo-name)

:::

---
